description: Categories for how commit messages express What changed and Why a skill modification was made. Independent of the skill-modification (pattern) taxonomy. Ported from the legacy RQ5 commit-message codebook (configs/commit_message_patterns.yaml).

what_expression_categories.coding_rule: Assign every What expression category supported by the commit messages. Use missing-what only when no What information is present.

allowed.label: summarize-skill-object-change
allowed.definition: Names the changed skill, skill file, resource, section, metadata field, script, or other skill artifact.

allowed.label: illustrate-skill-function
allowed.definition: Describes the changed skill behavior, agent capability, workflow effect, or user-facing function.

allowed.label: describe-skill-implementation-principle
allowed.definition: Explains the technical mechanism, execution principle, or implementation strategy used by the skill change.

allowed.label: missing-what
allowed.definition: Does not provide enough information to determine what changed in the skill.

why_expression_categories.coding_rule: Assign every Why expression category supported by the commit messages. Use missing-why only when no Why information is present.

allowed.label: describe-skill-issue
allowed.definition: States a bug, error, failure, limitation, inconsistency, or other problem in a skill or its supporting artifacts.

allowed.label: illustrate-skill-requirement
allowed.definition: States an external requirement, upstream change, platform constraint, user need, or usage scenario that required the skill change.

allowed.label: describe-skill-objective
allowed.definition: States the intended goal, benefit, improvement, or desired outcome of the skill change.

allowed.label: imply-skill-necessity
allowed.definition: Implies the need for the skill change without explicitly stating an issue, requirement, or objective.

allowed.label: missing-why
allowed.definition: Does not provide enough information to determine why the skill change was made.
